Apothecary L1 (H).name: APOTHECARY
Apothecary L1 (H).interrupt: Blinding Smoke on Primary 1s and 4s -2 damage / Glowing Shield on Secondary 2s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: GAS CLOUD
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -2 + special - you may not attack next turn

Apothecary L1 (H).flavour: The apothecary wears breeches and a leather tunic which they open to reveal a collection of vials. Pulling one free they throw it at you as they attack.
Apothecary L1 (H).mishap: One of the vials smashes at your feet but nothing happens. You gain an extra attack.
Apothecary L1 (H).prime: They retrieve a larger bottle from their belt. Flames burst up around you. Lose D6 HP.

Artisan L1 (H).name: ARTISAN
Artisan L1 (H).interrupt: Deflect on Secondary 1s -2 damage / Distract on Secondary 6s -1 damage
Artisan L1 (H).flavour: A skilled worker who has spent many years learning their art. They are not a fighter but are well coordinated and wear sturdy leather work clothes. They will defend their home.
Artisan L1 (H).mishap: They grab up a length of wood, but it is brittle and crumbles in their hand. Gain an extra attack.
Artisan L1 (H).prime: The artisan pulls a handful of pins from a pocket and throws them in your face. Take 2 damage.

Blacksmith L1 (H).name: BLACKSMITH
Blacksmith L1 (H).interrupt: Crossed Arms on Secondary 3s and 6s -2 damage
Blacksmith L1 (H).flavour: A hardy artisan, wearing a heavy leather apron, used to hammering metal so they are strong and resistant.
Blacksmith L1 (H).mishap: As the blacksmith attacks they catch their hammer in their apron. You kick out and cause D3 damage.
Blacksmith L1 (H).prime: The blacksmith pulls out a large hook and throws it at you. It catches your arm. Lose D3 HP.

Crazed Preacher L1 (H).name: CRAZED PREACHER
Crazed Preacher L1 (H).flavour: Spreading their vile words in amongst the inhabitants of the dungeon, these crazed preachers are full of fury. They scream and rush towards you.
Crazed Preacher L1 (H).mishap: The wild preacher becomes caught up in their robes and misses a round of combat.
Crazed Preacher L1 (H).prime: They jump forward and somehow latch on to you and are able to perform WILD SCRATCH twice.

Cultist L1 (H).name: CULTIST
Cultist L1 (H).flavour: Serving the religious leaders in the dungeon these worshippers are twisted by hate and set to evil tasks. They wear red robes and face paint, their eyes bulging as they attack.
Cultist L1 (H).mishap: The Cultist pulls a dagger and throws it but it falls at your feet. You throw it back for 2 damage.
Cultist L1 (H).prime: They pull a long chain and fling it at you and wrap it around you arm causing 1 damage.

Dark Cleric L1 (H).name: DARK CLERIC
Dark Cleric L1 (H).interrupt: Dark Magic Haze on Secondary 1s, 2s and 3s - the Dark Cleric heals 1 point of damage if injured and gains one point if not.
Dark Cleric L1 (H).flavour: These brooding magic wielders worship Maduva and harness the power of the dead, turning it upon their enemies. They wear textured grey and black cloaks and padded armour.
Dark Cleric L1 (H).mishap: The cleric swings his staff but misses and slips from their hands. Gain 1 extra attack.
Dark Cleric L1 (H).prime: The air turns cold as the cleric raises their staff. You feel a chill run through you. Lose 2 HP.

Fungal Geist L1 (F).name: FUNGAL GEIST
Fungal Geist L1 (F).flavour: A squat fungal humanoid, part mushroom part unknown beast. Their appearance can vary, but they usually have a wide textured cap and small mushrooms sometimes cover their torso.
Fungal Geist L1 (F).mishap: The geist attempts a volley of kicks all of which fail short allowing you to prod it hard causing 2 damage.
Fungal Geist L1 (F).prime: The geist raises its cap revealing narrow gills from which it fires a series of darts. Lose D3 HP.

Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).name: GIANT HORNED ANACONDA
Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).interrupt: Scale block on Secondary 2s and 5s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: SMOTHER
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -2 damage + special attack - miss next round (once per combat).

Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).flavour: A large brown snake with rock like horned ridges, it uses to attack aggressors. They live in rocky environments often near dungeons or caves, where their appearance is perfect camouflage.
Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).mishap: The snake snaps back and tries to bash you with its head, but instead smashes the wall for D3 damage.
Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).prime: The snake rises high on its tail and swoops down performing a successful HORN JAB.

Guard L1 (H).name: GUARD
Guard L1 (H).interrupt: Shield Block on Secondary 3s and 5s -1 damage
Guard L1 (H).flavour: A trained guard wearing light armour who has some basic skills in combat. They have duties within the dungeon and can be seen on patrols and at guard posts.
Guard L1 (H).mishap: The guard lunges carelessly, opens his side and you elbow them hard causing 1 damage.
Guard L1 (H).prime: With an unexpected move the guard disarms you. Lose 1 turn as you grab it back up off the ground.

Guard Dog L1 (A).name: GUARD DOG
Guard Dog L1 (A).flavour: A trained guard dog, appears vicious but has little experience fighting.
Guard Dog L1 (A).mishap: The dog skids as it lunges and you are able to get in an extra attack with +1.
Guard Dog L1 (A).prime: The dog thrusts forward and clamps its jaws around your arm causing D3 damage.

Huge Infected Rat L1 (A).name: HUGE INFECTED RAT

manoeuvres.name: INFECTED BITE
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -3 damage + special - next two turns lose 1 HP

Huge Infected Rat L1 (A).flavour: A large grim looking rodent covered in pus-filled blisters that appears to be in a rabid state. Its red eyes bulge as it screeches and attacks.
Huge Infected Rat L1 (A).mishap: The creature is sickly and seems to cough and spits blood causing it D3 damage.
Huge Infected Rat L1 (A).prime: The rat evades your defences and scurries up your leg and bites your neck using INFECTED BITE.

Huge Rat L1 (A).name: HUGE RAT
Huge Rat L1 (A).treasure: A rat tail without becoming bloodied.
Huge Rat L1 (A).flavour: Not a giant but bigger than a normal rat that will savagely attack anything, even larger creatures, when hungry. They are always hungry.
Huge Rat L1 (A).mishap: The rat attacks but slips and rolls on its back. You bring down your boot and crush its head, killing it.
Huge Rat L1 (A).prime: The rat pounces and lands on your face. It bites you before you brush it off. Lose 1 HP.

Huge Spider L1 (I).name: HUGE SPIDER
Huge Spider L1 (I).flavour: Not a giant but bigger than a normal spider with a tough carapace and enlarged fangs. It will attack if something breaks its web.
Huge Spider L1 (I).mishap: The spider falls as its web breaks and you spin and slice it in two, killing it instantly.
Huge Spider L1 (I).prime: The spider jumps, spins a web, and pings onto your back where it sinks in its fangs. Lose 1 HP.

Infernal Monk L1 (H).name: INFERNAL MONK
Infernal Monk L1 (H).interrupt: Heat Wave on Primary 2s and 5s -1 damage
Infernal Monk L1 (H).flavour: Dressed in red leather bands and black clothes the bald monk can call upon Grakada to summon fire that extends from their arm as an infernal whip. It lashes towards you as they attack.
Infernal Monk L1 (H).mishap: Their whip lashes the air and flings back in their face causing them 2 damage.
Infernal Monk L1 (H).prime: A second fire whip appears on their other hand and together they strike you. Lose 3 HP.

Jailor L1 (H).name: JAILOR
Jailor L1 (H).flavour: Being a jailor has made them tough but their skill in combat is limited. As they come forward wrapping a chain around their knuckles, a mad look in their eyes.
Jailor L1 (H).mishap: The jailor swings but appears tired and falls back on their haunches giving you an extra attack.
Jailor L1 (H).prime: The chain shoots out and lashes your knuckles. Lose 1 HP if not wearing gloves.

Labourer L1 (H).name: LABOURER
Labourer L1 (H).flavour: Poorly equipped and unarmed, this labourer offers little challenge, but they have learned patience and do a range of jobs around the dungeon which they will fiercely defend.
Labourer L1 (H).mishap: The labourer flays wildly and loses their balance. You quickly counter gaining an extra attack.
Labourer L1 (H).prime: The labourer barrels past your defence swinging wildly and a punch lands. Take 1 damage.

Medic L1 (H).name: MEDIC
Medic L1 (H).interrupt: Nimble Move on Primary 3s and 4s -1 damage
Medic L1 (H).flavour: The nearest thing the dungeon has to a herbalist medic who looks after the injured but has very little skill. From a bloody apron they pull out a short knife and attack.
Medic L1 (H).mishap: There must be blood on the medic's shoes for they lose their footing and fall. Gain 1 extra attack.
Medic L1 (H).prime: The medic whips the apron free, throws it over your head and stabs you causing 2 damage.

Merchant L1 (H).name: MERCHANT
Merchant L1 (H).treasure: A wallet with D6 GC and D6 SC
Merchant L1 (H).interrupt: Divert Blow on Primary 2s and 5s -1 damage
Merchant L1 (H).flavour: Dressed in fine clothes the merchant manages trade in the dungeon and makes a profit doing so. They pull out a curved sword and prepare to defend their position.
Merchant L1 (H).mishap: They try a flurry of complex swings, trip and slash their own leg. They take one damage.
Merchant L1 (H).prime: The merchant throws a potion at you that explodes. It causes D3 damage.

Musty Bloater L1 (F).name: MUSTY BLOATER
Musty Bloater L1 (F).interrupt: Blinding Spore Cloud on Primary 3s, 4s and 5s -1 damage
Musty Bloater L1 (F).flavour: Animated by primordial magic these swollen fungi shoot hot spores at their victims. They lumber forward, rising up and swelling, from what appears to be flat mould or mildew.
Musty Bloater L1 (F).mishap: The creature seems to lose some of its mass and shrinks for a moment. It misses a round of combat.
Musty Bloater L1 (F).prime: Suddenly the Bloater pressure shoots out a shower of spores. Lose D3 HP.

Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).name: SCARLET COBRA
Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).interrupt: Darts Aside on Primary 2s and 3s -1 damage

manoeuvres.name: BITE
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -3 damage + special attack - poisoned - lose 1 HP per round for 2 rounds.

Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).flavour: A poisonous black snake with scarlet red banding. Lives in deep burrows sometimes finding its way down through cracks in the walls and ceiling of the dungeon.
Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).mishap: The snake shoots out its head in an attempted bite, but misses and you're able to slash it for D3 damage.
Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).prime: The cobra corners coils and whips forward past your defence successfully biting you, see BITE.

Scout L1 (H).name: SCOUT
Scout L1 (H).interrupt: Swift Jump on Primary 1s and 6s -2 damage
Scout L1 (H).flavour: Usually out of the dungeon scouting the area they strive to avoid combat but are not afraid of a fight. Dressed in green and agile on their feet they thrust the point of a dagger towards you.
Scout L1 (H).mishap: The scout tries to grab your weapon but instead twists their wrist and takes 1 damage.
Scout L1 (H).prime: The scout leaps back, pulls out a short bow and looses an arrow. Take D3 damage.

Shard Rock Python L1 (A).name: SHARD ROCK PYTHON

manoeuvres.name: STRANGLE
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -1 damage + special attack - when you attack next round you do one less damage.

Shard Rock Python L1 (A).flavour: A heavy snake, with textured rough skin that is a grey rock colour. It wraps around its victims and strangles the life out of them.
Shard Rock Python L1 (A).mishap: The snake's slow attack allows you to bash it on the head as it tries to move in closer causing D6 damage.
Shard Rock Python L1 (A).prime: The python corners you, wraps it's bulk around your leg causing lose D6 HP.

Slimy Gorger L1 (F).name: SLIMY GORGER
Slimy Gorger L1 (F).interrupt: Gelatinous Body on Secondary 2s and 6s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: STICKY SUCTION
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -1 damage + special attack - you may not use one random piece of armour this turn.

Slimy Gorger L1 (F).flavour: A dark green slime animated by primordial magic, that lives in wet and cold abandoned spaces. It can rear up and attack with its stretchy gelatinous body.
Slimy Gorger L1 (F).mishap: The slime hisses, but cannot take an attacking form and misses a round of combat.
Slimy Gorger L1 (F).prime: The slime swells into a wide sheet of green that engulfs you. You struggle free but lose 2 HP.

Thug L1 (H).name: THUG
Thug L1 (H).flavour: This rough looking brute has few skills and little training and is often deployed as muscle to do unsavoury jobs. They shout abuse as they attack swinging their fists.
Thug L1 (H).mishap: The thug tries a combo of swing and uppercut but misses and topples over. Gain 1 extra attack.
Thug L1 (H).prime: The thug manages to grip and up eyes wide they headbutt you hard, take D3 damage.

Veteran L1 (H).name: VETERAN
Veteran L1 (H).treasure: Roll on PT1 +1 and an extra D6 GC
Veteran L1 (H).interrupt: Parry on Primary 3s and 4s -2 damage / Armour deflection on Secondary 5s and 6s -1 damage
Veteran L1 (H).flavour: A strong and experienced old fighter, who used to be a soldier, and knows how to handle themselves. They appear grizzled and slow but can parry efficiently. They sneer as they attack.
Veteran L1 (H).mishap: The old veteran stumbles and falls. Gain 2 extra hits as he tries to rise.
Veteran L1 (H).prime: Out of nowhere the veteran twists and performs a successful CRUSHING BLOW.

War Hound L1 (A).name: WAR HOUND
War Hound L1 (A).interrupt: Raised Legs on Primary 5s and 6s -2 damage
War Hound L1 (A).flavour: A vicious dog trained to fight. Wears a spiked collar it uses in its charge attack.
War Hound L1 (A).mishap: The dog seems keen on simply barking in an attempt to scare you and misses the next round.
War Hound L1 (A).prime: The dog barrels into you dragging its spiked collar down your thigh causing D6 damage.

Warrior L1 (H).name: WARRIOR
Warrior L1 (H).interrupt: Bracer block on Primary 4s -1 damage / Dodge on Secondary 3s - 1 damage
Warrior L1 (H).flavour: A capable warrior trained in the art of combat. They move quickly and  appear well equipped. They wear thick metal bracers which they can use to block attacks.
Warrior L1 (H).mishap: The warrior swings but loses his balance and you kick them hard in the ribs causing D3 damage.
Warrior L1 (H).prime: The warrior pulls a small dagger from his boot and flicks it at you. It glances off your cheek. Lose 1 HP.

Work Dog L1 (A).name: WORK DOG
Work Dog L1 (A).flavour: A working dog with a need to defend its owner, and although not trained to fight still has a savage bite.
Work Dog L1 (A).mishap: The dog flinches and slips as it comes forward afraid of your attack. It hits the wall taking 1 damage.
Work Dog L1 (A).prime: The dog wrestles through your defence and slashes you with a paw. Lose 1 HP.

Handler L1 (H).name: HANDLER
Handler L1 (H).treasure: A pouch full of biscuit chips.
Handler L1 (H).interrupt: Leather Straps on Secondary 4s and 6s -1 damage
Handler L1 (H).flavour: The handler appears dirty and their clothes worn and muddy. Dealing with the dogs and cleaning out the kennels is their primary role which is clear from their apperance. They carry a whip.
Handler L1 (H).mishap: Caught in two minds, an inexperienced fighter, the handler hesitates and misses their next attack.
Handler L1 (H).prime: The handler whoops and cracks their whip. Like a flash you feel it lash your cheek and lose 1 HP.
